mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean excess returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, with Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during tightening periods, with a negative Rank IC exceeding -0.04 over consecutive 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing market volatility and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 300 consistently coincides with positive 5-day forward returns, and the signal predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside elevated federal funds rates signals supply-side constraints meeting restrictive monetary policy, triggering recession fears and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO declines and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, with the joint signal showing no predictive edge (Rank IC < 0.02) over rolling windows.
